_source: Synthesized from canonical microbiology references — E. coli Nissle 1917 (CGAT-Genomes / Sonnenburg lab toolkit reviews); Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron VPI-5482 reference genome; Synlogic SYNB1934 / Synlogic-class engineered EcN precedents; F. prausnitzii A2-165 reference

E_coli_Nissle_1917.O2_in_colon_lumen: essentially anoxic but EcN tolerates microaerobic and brief aerobic exposures; cell envelope adaptation supports a small O2 flux through respiratory chain

native_aromatic_amino_acid_supply.Hpg_4_hydroxyphenylglycine: no native pathway; must be supplied by cluster's hmaS/hmo/hpgT/pd
native_aromatic_amino_acid_supply.betaOHTyr: no native pathway; must be supplied by OxyD-type beta-hydroxylase

E_coli_Nissle_1917.PPTase_native: AcpS (housekeeping; ACP-specific, NOT broad NRPS-PCP active)
E_coli_Nissle_1917.PPTase_required_addition: Sfp from B. subtilis or actinomycete Sfp-type (PcpS, KirP) — well-documented integration into E. coli chromosomal lacZ or rhaBAD locus
E_coli_Nissle_1917.P450_redox_partner_compatibility: needs heterologous ferredoxin/ferredoxin-reductase coexpression OR P450 fusion to reductase domain (e.g., BM3-style); E. coli endogenous redox does not pair productively with most Streptomyces P450s
E_coli_Nissle_1917.engineering_toolkit_maturity: very high — pSC101/pBR322 replicons, lambda-Red recombineering, MAGE, CRISPR-Cas9, RBS Calculator, well-characterized inducible promoters (P_BAD, P_T7, P_tet, P_rha), Synlogic-class genome integration via auxotrophic complementation

Bacteroides_thetaiotaomicron_VPI_5482.strict_anaerobe: tolerates brief O2 exposure (10-60 min) but cannot grow with O2; intracellular O2 partial pressure during growth essentially zero
Bacteroides_thetaiotaomicron_VPI_5482.PPTase_native: type II FAS housekeeping AcpS-like; no characterized Sfp-type broad-specificity PPTase
Bacteroides_thetaiotaomicron_VPI_5482.PPTase_required_addition: yes (no precedent for Bacteroides NRPS megacluster expression; would need anaerobe-codon-optimized Sfp variant)
Bacteroides_thetaiotaomicron_VPI_5482.P450_redox_partner_compatibility: fundamentally incompatible — Bacteroides P450 inventory is sparse and not designed for NRPS-tailoring biosynthesis; more importantly the P450 reaction itself requires O2
Bacteroides_thetaiotaomicron_VPI_5482.engineering_toolkit_maturity: medium — Sonnenburg lab toolkit (NBU integration, anhydrotetracycline-inducible promoters, CRISPRi); no megacluster (>30 kb) heterologous expression precedent in any Bacteroides species

F_prausnitzii_A2_165._note: Listed for completeness only. F. prausnitzii is the preferred LBP chassis for butyrate-pathway boost (cf. engineered-lbp-chassis.md butyrate dual-action), but is not a candidate for complestatin BGC expression — same O2 incompatibility as Bacteroides plus more brittle genetics toolkit. Excluded from this analysis.

comparator_C1_INH_recombinant.source_paper_class: Sanofi Cinryze / Pharming Ruconest / various Pichia and CHO programs
comparator_C1_INH_recombinant.protein_class: single-chain serpin, 478 aa mature, heavily N- and O-glycosylated
FDA_approved_versions: recombinant rabbit-milk transgenic (Ruconest / conestat alfa, Pharming)
comparator_C1_INH_recombinant.Pichia_pastoris_titer_g_per_L: ~0.5-2 g/L reported in developmental literature; functional inhibitory activity equivalent to plasma C1-INH after pichia-glycoengineering
comparator_C1_INH_recombinant.E_coli_feasibility: very low — heavily glycosylated; aglycosylated recombinant C1-INH from E. coli is poorly secreted and rapidly cleared in vivo (similar pattern to other serpins)
comparator_C1_INH_recombinant.Bacteroides_feasibility: no precedent; obligate-anaerobe Bacteroides cannot perform mammalian-class N-glycosylation; same fundamental limitation
comparator_C1_INH_recombinant.E_coli_Nissle_LBP_feasibility: low for the secreted-protein angle but the chassis_question_is_different: C1-INH delivered as a continuous LBP-secreted payload would not need the half-life optimization that plasma-injected versions need. Could potentially work as a luminal-released payload but is heavily protease-vulnerable in the gut.
comparator_C1_INH_recombinant.engineering_complexity_relative_to_compl: much lower — single gene, no tailoring enzymes, no non-canonical amino acids, no megacluster, no O2-dependent biosynthesis chemistry. Glycosylation is the dominant feasibility question, and the LBP chassis dodges the systemic-half-life problem that drives the Pichia glycoengineering effort.

feasibility_scoring_rubric._note: Each factor scored 0.0 (catastrophic / cannot proceed) to 1.0 (no obstacle). Geometric mean across all factors = overall feasibility score. Cutoffs: >=0.60 GREEN; 0.30-0.59 YELLOW; <0.30 RED.
